Lawn Care Tailored to Raleigh

Expert lawn services designed for the Triangle's unique climate and soil. Keeping your yard healthy from Durham to Apex with Piedmont-specific solutions.

Call Now: 555-law-r91

Get a FREE Estimate Today!

Rooted in Raleigh

Founded in 2012, Summit Lawn Care grew from a single truck serving NC State faculty neighborhoods into Wake County's most trusted lawn service. We've seen Raleigh transform from tobacco town to tech hub, adapting our methods to new construction yards and historic properties alike.

Since 2014 Established
Locally Owned

Our Lawn Care Services

Professional lawn care solutions tailored to your needs.

Red Clay Lawn Aeration

Core aeration designed for Raleigh's compacted clay soils.

Emergency response for fallen limbs and debris after Triangle storms.

Learn More →

Turf Builder Program

Frequently Asked Questions

Get answers to the most common questions about our lawn care services.

Why can't I just spike aerate myself?

Spikes compact clay sideways creating more problems. Our core removal method is the only proven solution for Piedmont soils.

How often should Raleigh lawns be aerated?

Most properties need annual service, but newer construction yards with heavy equipment compaction may benefit from biannual treatments.

Reviews From Our Lawn Care Customers

See what our satisfied customers have to say about their experience.

"After three failed attempts with other companies, Summit finally fixed our standing water issues near the Crabtree watershed. Their deep plugs and sand mix made an immediate difference - our backyard no longer turns into a swamp after summer storms. The technician even pointed out where the builder had buried construction debris that was causing drainage problems."

Marshall T. Red Clay Lawn Aeration

"Moving from California, we had no idea how to deal with Raleigh's red clay. Summit's soil prep and custom seed blend gave us a lush lawn by Thanksgiving despite starting in September. Their follow-up visits caught early fungus spots before they spread, saving us thousands compared to our neighbors who had to resod."

Priya K. New Construction Lawn Establishment

"Finding someone who understands Oakwood's preservation requirements was a challenge until Summit. They revived our 1920s ribbon lawn using archival photos and hand-tied the new edging to match original patterns. Most importantly, their light equipment hasn't damaged the antique brick walkways like previous services did."

Eleanor W. Historic Home Lawn Care
